body.title: CLOSED: Dreaming pass: cluster pe_chain outcomes by tag, write procedure to palace for 3+ successes
body.result: schema extraction in run(): _closed_tickets_by_tag() + _synthesize_sprint_pattern() + _palace_write(); 1 node per pass, 20s timeout
body.description:
Igor's dreaming module produces reflections but doesn't extract reusable procedures from experience. Hippocampal schema formation analog: when the same type of successful sprint is observed 3+ times, extract the common pattern as a palace procedure node so future sprints of the same type benefit from prior experience.

In the dreaming pass, query cc_queue for closed tickets grouped by primary tag (last 60 days). For any tag with 3+ successful closes, synthesize via LLM: 'For [tag] tickets, the successful approach is [summary from closed ticket descriptions].' Write to palace at path theigors/procedures/[tag]-sprint-pattern. Skip tags already documented (check palace before writing).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/dreaming.py (MEDIUM inertia); palace writes via memory MCP tools
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia); theigors/rules/memory (palace writes, not new memory types); no sqlite
**Scope boundary:** In scope: tag-based clustering of closed tickets + LLM synthesis + palace write for tags with 3+ successes. Out of scope: failure pattern extraction (that's T-igor-dreaming-outputs-actionable); changes to dreaming reflection prompt.
**Test plan:** With 3+ closed tickets tagged 'Cognition' in cc_queue, dreaming pass writes palace node at theigors/procedures/cognition-sprint-pattern. memory_get(path='theigors/procedures/cognition-sprint-pattern') returns content. Node not duplicated on subsequent dreaming passes.
